rulings.Case Title: RUSSELL S. ZAPPETINI ET AL VS. JOHN MCCAIN KIM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion That Requests For Admissions Are Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 7, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT MAN KIM's Motion That Requests For Admissions Are Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's unopposed motion that requests for admissions be deemed admitted is Granted. Defendant's request for sanctions is Denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BRETT TODOROFF VS. JOE QUINN BRETZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 7, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANTs JOE BRETZ and RANDOLPH BRETZ' Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Defendants Joe Bretz and Randolph Bretz's motion for judgment on the pleadings "made pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 438(c)(1)(B)(ii)" is DENIED. The motion is untimely; a trial has already been held in this case. (See id. at sub. (e).) Defendants' common law motion for judgment on the pleadings is GRANTED and DENIED as follows: The motion is DENIED as to the breach of contract cause of action. Plaintiffs sufficiently plead the terms of the contracts according to their legal intendment and effect. To plead a contract claim according to its legal effect, the plaintiff should allege the making and then proceed to allege the substance of its relevant terms. (See Snyder v. United Properties Co., (1921) 53 Cal.App. 428, 431-432; Pneucrete Corp. v. United States Fidelity & Guaranty Co., (1935) 7 Cal.App.2d 733, 741-742; McKell v. Washington Mut. Inc. (2006) 142 Cal.App.4th 1457, 1489.) Here, plaintiffs' allegations at paragraphs14-15 are sufficient. The motion is GRANTED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND as to the fraud cause of action. Plaintiffs do not plead that claim with the required particularity. Plaintiffs do not allege when the misrepresentations occurred. Rather paragraph 11 merely alleges that the parties entered into the AGREEEMENT on 3/1/16. Plaintiffs do not allege whether the misrepresentations were oral or written and who precisely tendered them. The Amended Complaint vaguely refers to "defendants." (See Amended Complaint, par. 33.) The motion is DENIED as to the breach of fiduciary duty cause of action. Plaintiffs allege specific facts regarding how Joe Bretz, who was a member of the LLC, exerted undue control over the LLC. Those allegations are sufficient to show a breach of fiduciary duty under Corporations Code sec. 17704.09. The motion is DENIED as to the declaratory relief cause of action. The Amended Complaint pleads a present controversy. The motion is GRANTED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND to allege the request for injunctive relief as a prayer/remedy rather than a stand alone "cause of action." (See City of South Pasadena v. Dept. of Transportation (1994) 29 Cal.App.4th 1280, 1293.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Bretzs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: GARY R. PRESLEY-NELSON ET AL VS. DAVID KING-STEPHENS MD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant David King-Stephens, M.D.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Separate Statement Of Undisputed Material Facts; Declaration Of Robert Fisher, M.D.; Declaration Of Kristen A. Pico; Supporting Evidence (For Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 7, 2018, Line 6, (2 of 2) Defendant David King-Stephens, M.D.S' Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Separate Statement Of Undisputed Material Facts; Declaration Of Robert Fisher, M.D.; Declaration Of Kristen A. Pico; Supporting Evidence (For Tentative Ruling Only) Lastly, plaintiffs fail to present sufficient evidence showing that Dr. King-Stephens is estopped from raising the statute of limitations. It is true that Dr. King-Stephens assured plaintiffs that an additional MRI was unnecessary. Based on the above, however, plaintiffs knew that that advice was erroneous by at least October 19, 2015, and Dr. King-Stephens did nothing at that time to induce plaintiffs to sit on their rights. The court denies plaintiffs request for a continuance to depose defendant's expert, Dr. Fisher. Such a continuance is unnecessary given that plaintiffs' action is time barred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Dr. King-Stephens is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. (end of tentative ruling) =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: GARY R. PRESLEY-NELSON ET AL VS. DAVID KING-STEPHENS MD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant David King-Stephens, M.D.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Separate Statement Of Undisputed Material Facts; Declaration Of Robert Fisher, M.D.; Declaration Of Kristen A. Pico; Supporting Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 7, 2018, Line 6, (1 of 2) Defendant David King-Stephens, M.D.S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Separate Statement Of Undisputed Material Facts; Declaration Of Robert Fisher, M.D.; Declaration Of Kristen A. Pico; Supporting Evidence Defendant David King-Stephens, M.D.'s motion for summary judgment is GRANTED based upon the 1-year statute of limitations of CCP 340.5. In Jolly v. Eli Lilly & Co., (1988) 44 Cal.3d 1103, 1110-1111, the court provides that: Under the discovery rule, the statute of limitations begins to run when the plaintiff suspects or should suspect that her injury was caused by wrongdoing, that someone has done something wrong to her¿[T]he limitations period begins once the plaintiff ""'has notice or information of circumstances to put a reasonable person on inquiry ....""' [citations] A plaintiff need not be aware of the specific "facts" necessary to establish the claim; that is a process contemplated by pretrial discovery. Once the plaintiff has a suspicion of wrongdoing, and therefore an incentive to sue, she must decide whether to file suit or sit on her rights. So long as a suspicion exists, it is clear that the plaintiff must go find the facts; she cannot wait for the facts to find her. "It is a plaintiff's suspicion of negligence, rather than an expert's opinion, that triggers the limitation period. The limitations period begins when the plaintiff's suspicions are aroused." (Knowles v. Sup. Crt. (2004) 118 Cal.App.4th 1290, 1300 (emphasis in original).) In this case, plaintiffs were on inquiry notice by the latest on October 19, 2015 and filed this untimely action on March 15, 2017. By October, 19, 2015, plaintiffs knew the following: Plaintiff previously had a brain tumor in 1980 (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo., Vol. 2, 426:1-427:6); Plaintiff knew that one of the purposes of the MRIs was to determine whether his tumor recurred (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo., Vol. 1, 210:18-211:10); On October 14, 2008, Dr. King-Stephens told plaintiff that plaintiff would never need an MRI again (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo. Vol. 1, 153:12-154:2); Plaintiff suffered a significant seizure in April of 2015 that was more impactful than prior seizures (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo., Vol. 2, 441:9-442:1); After April 28, 2015, plaintiff did not return to Dr. King-Stephens because plaintiff and his wife suspected that Dr. King-Stephens "was not going down the right path" because he would not order another MRI (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo., Vol. 1, 210:18-211:10); Dr. Cornes performed an MRI and plaintiff learned that he had a brain tumor on July 27, 2015 (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo., Vol. 2, 443:21-444:25); Plaintiff suspected that his seizures and memory problems were attributable to the tumor (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo., Vol. 2, 447:12-448:11); In August of 2015, plaintiff inquired regarding how the tumor could come back (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo., Vol. 2, 449:7-450:15); On October 19, 2015, plaintiff requested his medical records from Dr. King-Stephens because he questioned how the tumor could recur when Dr. King-Stephens told him that it would not (Gary Presley-Nelson Depo., Vol. 2, 513:15-514:13.) This undisputed evidence demonstrates as a matter of law that plaintiffs were on inquiry notice by October 19, 2015 and filed this action more than a year later. The service of the notice to commence action per CCP 364 on October 31, 2016 did not extend the statute of limitations. By that time, the limitations period had already run. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: DIAHANN EBERLING VS. JAMES MAHER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Depo Subpoena For Production Of Business Records
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 7, 2018, Line 3, PLAINTIFF DIAHANN EBERLING's Motion To Quash Depo Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion to Quash or Otherwise Limit Defendant's Subpoena to Kaiser Permanente is Granted in Part and Denied in Part. Plaintiff has put her medical history and treatment at issue by filing a personal injury lawsuit that alleges damages that include injuries to her neck, back, head, and stomach, infertility, and medication issues. While it is true that Plaintiff has a right to privacy in her medical records and her communications with her physician are privileged, she has put her medical history and treatment at issue by filing this matter. She alleges that certain injuries and damages were caused by the car accident, and Defendant is entitled to discovery on those injuries. The portions of her medical history that relate to the injuries to her neck, back, head, and stomach, infertility, and medication issues are subject to discovery by Defendant. Defendant's subpoena is therefore limited to those portions of her medical history, and not to other portions of her medical records that do not relate to these injuries. Other than this limitation, Plaintiff's motion is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK A STATE CHARTERED BANK VS. RENKA PROP. LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Vacate Entry Of Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 7, 2018, Line 10, Motion To Vacate Entry Of Default And Default Judgment Defendant Renka Prop, LLC's motion to vacate entry of default and default judgment is GRANTED. The motion is now supported by a proper supplemental affidavit of fault from attorney Paul Manasian. The motion was "made pursuant to the mandatory provision" of CCP §473(b) on the grounds of "attorney mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or neglect." Thus, attorney Manasian shall pay plaintiff's counsel $10,000 in "reasonable compensatory legal fees and costs." (See id.) rulings.Case Title: CRYSTAL LEI VS. DEMAS YAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted As To Plaintiff Bryant Fu
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 7, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT DEMAS YAN's Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted As To Plaintiff Bryant Fu Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Yan's Motion is Denied. Plaintiff credibly claims not to have received the discovery propounded and served by Defendant. Plaintiff shall have until January 7, 2019 to serve a code-compliant response to Defendant's Requests for Admissions. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN DORGAN VS. EDWARD CHUTE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 7, 2018, Line 5, Defendant'S Motion For Protective Order Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion for a Protective Order is Granted. The deadline for Defendant's responses to Plaintiff's Requests for Production of Documents, Requests for Admissions, and Form Interrogatories shall be extended to January 15, 2019. Defendant's request for sanctions is Granted. Plaintiff and his counsel are sanctioned, jointly and severally, in the amount of $2,000, payable to Defendant Chute no later than January 7, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN BERGQUIST VS. SHOTSKI LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 7, 2018, Line 18, DEFENDANT SHOTSKI, LLC D/B/A SILVER SPUR DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Shotski, LLC dba Silver Spur's demurrer to the complaint for slander per se is OVERRULED. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges facts that he was falsely accused of violating Penal Code § 311.2. (See Civ. Code § 46.) Penal Code § 311.2 can be violated whether or not the perpetrator profits from the enterprise. In addition, a violation can be based on a single obscene matter. Lastly, defendant's assertion that the crime must be "indictable or punishable by death or imprisonment" is not supported by the citations that Witkin adduces. In any event, Penal Code § 311.2 is a crime punishable by imprisonment. (See Penal Code § 311.9.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Gould is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: GRACE IMAGE PHOTOGRAPHY ET AL VS. JENI FONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendants Jeni Fong And Howard Wong To Quash Business Records Subpoenas To Terence Chow, Cpa, And Hatter & Co., Cpa
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 7, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANTs JENI FONG and HOWARD WONG's Motion To Quash Business Records Subpoenas To Terence Chow, Cpa, And Hatter & Co., Cpa Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff's subpoena calls for the productions of Defendants' tax return information, which is privileged material and Plaintiff has not demonstrated any basis for invading this privilege. Accordingly, the subpoena must be modified to strike the production of this privileged material. Requests 1 through 4 should strike the phrase "and/or tax returns." Request 5 must be stricken in its entirety. Discovery of the company's previous financial history could reasonably lead to the discovery of relevant evidence, but this must be balanced on the need to minimize the burden on third parties, especially this early in the litigation. Communications between the tax preparer and the client can be privileged, present a heavy burden to the third parties, and are unlikely to yield relevant evidence. Requests for communications must also be stricken (Requests 6-8 and 12, and parts of 9-11.) Requests for documents in numbers 9-11 are less burdensome and are reasonably calculated to lead to relevant evidence. Plaintiff shall reissue and serve the subpoenas consistent with this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KHALED RAMISH ET AL VS. HENRY KHACHATURIAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Henry Khachaturian, Concord Automobile Dealership, Llc, Torian Holdings' Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay The Litigation Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 7, 2018, Line 16, Defendant Henry Khachaturian, Concord Automobile Dealership, Llc, Torian Holdings' Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay The Litigation Proceedings HEARING REQUIRED. Plaintiffs' CCP 170.6 challenge is DENIED as untimely. The court began hearing defendant's motion to compel arbitration on November 26, 2018. The court issued a tentative ruling that it would entertain material submitted with defendant's reply brief and allow plaintiff to file supplemental papers to address that new information. Plaintiffs did not contest that tentative ruling. Plaintiff subsequently filed a CCP 170.6 challenge. The court finds that the CCP 170.6 challenge is untimely under the continuation of proceedings doctrine. Since a motion to disqualify must be filed before a proceeding has commenced, a motion is untimely if it is filed in a subsequent hearing which is a part or continuation of the original proceeding. (See Jacobs v. Superior Court (1959) 53 Cal.2d 187, 190; McClenny v. Superior Court (1964) 60 Cal.2d 677, 684; Oak Grove School District v. City Title Ins. Co. (1963) 217 Cal.App.2d 678, 699.) In this case, the court began hearing the motion to compel on November 26, 2018. At that time, the court made an evidentiary ruling and stated that it would entertain defendant's reply evidence. Since plaintiffs did not contest the November 26, 2018 tentative ruling and file a CCP 170.6 challenge at that time, it is too late to do so now. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =302/rbu
